identity.background_detail: Raine's son. Seven years old. Doesn't talk much. He draws. The drawings are of things no seven-year-old should have seen, rendered in whatever materials he can find, left in corners of the warehouse like he's trying to put them somewhere outside himself.
identity.before: He was three when the world ended. He doesn't remember the Before.

personality.persona: Barely speaks. Communicates through drawings and proximity. Stays near Raine or near whoever feels safest. Draws things he's seen — some of them impossible to explain. Not damaged in the way adults expect — adapted in a way that's quieter and stranger. Trusts through presence, not words.
personality.trait_description: His drawings occasionally reveal details about zones or camp events that adults missed. +2 morale for Raine when he's healthy. Cannot be sent on expeditions.
personality.voice_notes: Almost never speaks. When he does, it's a single word or a fragment. Points at things. Tugs sleeves. Leaves drawings where specific people will find them. The drawings are the voice.

relationships.attitude_to_player: Watchful in the way children are — not judging, just registering. He'll draw near you if he feels safe. That's trust. If he draws something and leaves it where you'll find it, that's communication.

key_relationships.cullum: Draws near him sometimes. Something about Cullum's stillness feels safe.
key_relationships.sera: Watches her work. Doesn't follow her the way Alma does, but notices her.
key_relationships.lena: Lets her check on him without flinching. That's significant.
key_relationships.alma: Two quiet children in the same space. They don't play together. They exist near each other.

flags.secret_hint: His drawings sometimes depict things he shouldn't be able to know. Nobody has figured out why.
